info.title: marketing API
info.description: OpenAPI 3.1 document for the `marketing` module, generated from FCG InterfaceSpecs.

post.summary: Public form submission → create a Lead
post.description:
Public form submission → create a Lead.

POST /api/marketing/preview/:account/:feature/:id/submit

The public preview page posts the collected field values here. For the
`forms` feature we resolve the form, then create a `leads` record from the
submitted First Name / Last Name / Email (the default lead-capture fields
seeded on every form). Every other lead field is optional (V20260603.1), so
a visitor only ever has to supply those three.

Body shape: `{ values: { firstName, lastName, email, ... } }`. We also
accept a bare values map for resilience.

post.summary: OAuth completion. Called by the SPA popup-callback page (`/auth/social-callback`),
post.description:
OAuth completion. Called by the SPA popup-callback page (`/auth/social-callback`),
NOT by the provider directly: the provider redirects the popup to the frontend
route, which reads `code` + `state` from the URL and POSTs them here. We
exchange the code for tokens, list + persist the connectable profiles, register
the live adapter, and return a JSON result the popup relays to its opener via
postMessage. This mirrors the identity OAuth flow (POST /auth/oauth/complete)
and avoids needing raw Express `res` access, which the module-framework route
layer does not provide to service methods.
